data.task:
Atom 6 — ship the single all-persona Routing Guide (founder-approved 2026-06-20). This is the last deliverable before this priority is archive-ready. Read these first: docs/oz-improvement-routing.md (the file to generalize), docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md (the owner-map drill-down), packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md (the 'Durable Orchestration Changes' section), and packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts (the enforcer to extend).

DELIVERABLE — make these four edits, no others:

1. GENERALIZE docs/oz-improvement-routing.md from Oz-only into the all-persona Routing Guide. It must own TWO axes explicitly:
   (a) Product-vs-workspace TARGET axis, as the FIRST cut: Product = packages/** (including packages/personas/base/**, templates, public docs, schemas, shipped prompts) → ships to EVERY repo; guarded by ADR-0012 portability + verified run/ADR/tests. Workspace = <ws.path>/cocoder/** → that repo only. State explicitly that self-host (CoCoder/cocoder/) and consumer ([repo]/cocoder/) are the SAME mechanism (<ws.path>/cocoder/**); the only discriminator is product-vs-workspace, NOT 'am I sitting in the CoCoder repo.' The ADR-0012 portability test IS the discriminator rule (if the rule still teaches the role with CoCoder nouns stripped → product/base; else → workspace delta).
   (b) Kind-of-change → single OWNER → WRITE-PATH axis.
   Keep the existing target vocabulary (cocoder-product, workspace-shared, workspace-local, install-local, upstream-candidate) and RETIRE the Oz-only scoping so it reads as all-persona (Oz/Oscar/Deb/Bob). Include the escape hatch: a change fitting no row, spanning owners, or reversing an Accepted ADR → surface to the founder; do not improvise a new path. Do NOT create a new routing file — generalize this existing one (a second routing file is the exact duplication this priority condemns).

2. ADD ONE trigger line to packages/personas/base/shared-standards.md in the 'Durable Orchestration Changes' section pointing every persona at the Routing Guide. One line, pointer model only — do NOT inline a routing table into the prompt.

3. ADD a one-line pointer in docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md noting the Routing Guide is the runtime routing layer and this owner map is its drill-down.

4. EXTEND packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts (do NOT add a parallel test file) with assertions that pin the guide's target taxonomy/category names so they cannot silently drift, AND that fail if a second routing-guide owner reappears (e.g. assert exactly one routing guide exists / the shared-standards trigger points at the single guide).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: These three suites must pass — run and paste output for each:
   - pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/personas/tests/base-personas.test.ts
   - pnpm --dir '/Volumes/NAS LOCAL/CoCoder' exec vitest run packages/core/tests/orchestration-contracts.test.ts
   - the core test suite (packages/core)

MUST NOT BREAK: existing base-persona tests; the existing target vocabulary; ADR-0012/0023/0026 distinctions (map them, do not reverse). Do not redesign runner/daemon/UI/commit-spine. Do not create any new file — all four edits are to existing files. Report the exact diff and the three test outputs.

data.reason: Atom 6 lands: oz-improvement-routing.md generalized to the all-persona two-axis Routing Guide (product-vs-workspace first cut + kind→owner→write-path, vocab kept, ADR-0012 discriminator, escape hatch), one trigger line in shared-standards, one-line pointer in the owner map, and orchestration-contracts.test.ts extended to pin the taxonomy + a single-routing-owner guard; only the four intended existing files changed; base-personas, orchestration-contracts (7 tests), and the core suite (441/441) all green.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 2 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md, docs/oz-improvement-routing.md.
